did: REPRODUCED the clinical-template bug on the corrected 997-evidence AI/labor corpus: the agentic outline emitted Mechanism/Efficacy/Safety/Regulatory/Population Subgroups/Long-term Outcomes because (a) compose never threaded domain into generate_multi_section_report (defaulted '' -> clinical _ALLOWED_SECTIONS) and (b) even the non-clinical facet path was a flat bag with no intro/synthesis/conclusion. Prior STEP-16 masked it with a hardcoded clinical->AI/labor relabel MAP + hardcoded intro/title (overfit).

FIXED at the source, general/structural: added DEFAULT-OFF flag PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON in multi_section_generator.py that wraps the emergent facet outline in the standard research-report skeleton (OPEN framing -> thematic bodies with EMERGENT titles -> Cross-Study Synthesis & Contradictions -> Conclusions & Research Gaps). Only the four structural ROLES are fixed; no topic/title/benchmark hardcoded. Clinical/unknown path byte-identical.

DE-OVERFIT the compose harness: threaded domain=corpus.domain, deleted the _ARCHETYPE_HEADINGS relabel map + _humanize_heading + hardcoded AI/labor intro/title, added _derive_title (strips leading imperatives from RQ), render section titles verbatim, topic-neutral framing sentence.

PROVED generality at outline-seed level across 3 domains (scripts/outline_generality_probe.py, one LLM call each): AI/labor, MEDICAL (gut microbiota), FINANCE (global insurers) ALL produce Introduction -> thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Synthesis/Contradictions -> Conclusions/Research Gaps, topic-appropriate, zero clinical leak.

RAN the full live agentic compose over the corrected 997-evidence/329-basket corpus: cp4_used=agentic (outline agent loop, 9 react turns, search_more_evidence + update_outline), 8 topic-driven sections ALL kept, faithfulness PASS (0 leaked [CITE:ev], 0 unresolved markers). Confirmed raw multi_section_outline.json has ZERO clinical archetypes.
SCORED on RACE task 72 (gpt-5.5 judge) and beat the 0.30 best-compose baseline on every dimension.
result.measurement: RACE task 72, gpt-5.5 judge, on outputs/step2_topic_structure/report.md (obtained via scripts/score_report_race.py, log outputs/step2_race_score.log): Comprehensiveness 0.4435 (baseline 0.3391, +0.104), Insight 0.4192 (0.2875, +0.132), Instruction Following 0.4389 (0.2775, +0.161), Readability 0.4245 (0.2966, +0.128), OVERALL 0.4317 (best-compose 0.3023, +0.129). Beats the 0.30 baseline on all four dimensions and edges the claude-3-7-sonnet reference (0.4218 overall) sitting in the results dir. Faithfulness gate PASS (leaked_[CITE:ev]=0). Generality: outputs/outline_generality_probe.json shows the same intro/thematic/synthesis-contradictions/conclusion-gaps skeleton on AI/labor + medical + finance.
result.exercised: Outline generality: ran scripts/outline_generality_probe.py live (real deepseek-v4-pro seed call) on 3 domains — printed the actual section plans (all topic-appropriate, no archetypes). Full pipeline: ran scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py live (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1, GLM-5.2) over the corrected 997-evidence corpus for ~30min -> real report.md with 8 topic-driven sections, cp4_used=agentic confirmed in logs (outline_agent update_outline/search_more_evidence turns). RACE: ran the official third_party/deep_research_bench RACE harness (gpt-5.5) end-to-end -> real race_result.txt. Not py_compile — real LLM runs and a real judged benchmark number.
open: Generality was proven at the OUTLINE-SEED level for medical/finance (one LLM call each), but only AI/labor got a full compose+RACE number (the other two DRB tasks have no pre-built corpus; building one needs the retrieval stack).

Minor cosmetic artifact in the rendered prose: adjacent citation markers occasionally render with a stray period ('[6].[7]'); pre-existing sentence-assembly behavior, not a faithfulness issue; did not chase this round.
0.4317 beats the stated 0.30 baseline and the sonnet reference, but true deep-research SOTA on RACE is higher (~0.45-0.50 overall); more headroom likely exists in per-section depth/insight.
result.needs_fable: Is the outline-seed-level generality proof on medical+finance (plus a full compose+RACE only on AI/labor, overall 0.4317 vs 0.30 baseline, +0.13 on every dimension, faithfulness PASS) sufficient to call the topic-driven structure fix DONE and generalized — or does the bar require a full compose+RACE on a SECOND-domain corpus (which means building a new corpus via the retrieval stack, a much larger lift), before we move on to squeezing more Insight depth?

result.gap_to_top: The four STATED success criteria are all met and verified with real measurement: (1) cp4_used=agentic on the full corrected 329-basket/1120-row corpus, never fallback (confirmed by live outline_agent search_more_evidence/update_outline turns); (2) topic-appropriate synthesis-enabling structure (Intro/Scope -> 5 emergent thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Synthesis & Contradictions -> Conclusions & Research Gaps, zero clinical archetypes in raw outline); (3) generality proven across AI/labor, medical, finance at the outline-seed level; (4) RACE comprehensiveness 0.4435 and instruction-following 0.4389 both beat the 0.30 baseline, all four dims +0.10..+0.16, faithfulness PASS. BUT the mission bar is the number AT THE TOP, and Opus itself cites deep-research SOTA at ~0.45-0.50 overall vs our 0.4317. The lowest dimension is INSIGHT 0.4192 — which is also the highest-weight RACE lever (0.32) and the mission's originally-diagnosed biggest gap. So the structural bug is fixed and generalized, but absolute top is not yet reached; proven headroom remains in per-section insight depth.
result.reason: Nothing is broken (correct + faithfulness_safe both true), so not FIX_REGRESSION. Not SIGN_OFF because at_top is false: 0.4317 beats the baseline and the sonnet reference but sits below the ~0.45-0.50 SOTA Opus cites, and Insight (0.4192, weight 0.32) is the weakest dimension and the mission's headline gap. On Opus's actual question: the outline-seed generality probe SATISFIES criterion (3) as written ('structure fix works across >=3 different-domain questions') — and, critically, the fix is a domain-independent structural prompt addendum (_FACET_SKELETON_ADDENDUM fixes only the four roles, no topic/title hardcoded), so a second full compose+RACE corpus would add little generality confidence for a large retrieval-stack lift, and chasing a second benchmark task risks re-introducing the exact overfitting the operator flagged. Do NOT build a second-domain corpus now. Spend the next wheel on Insight depth, the real remaining lever.
result.next_step: Do NOT build a second-domain RACE corpus (large lift, marginal generality gain, overfitting risk). Attack INSIGHT depth — the highest-weight lever (0.32) and lowest dim (0.4192). Concrete: 49 of 128 candidate sentences (38%) are dropped at the faithfulness fold-in gate, starving body/synthesis sections of verified quantitative contrasts. In src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py, focus the 'Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions' role: ensure it is ASSIGNED the evidence rows whose verified findings converge/conflict and PROMPTED to quantify agreement/disagreement across those verified numbers (comparing the [CITE:ev]-backed figures the fold-in already accepts), rather than restating body claims. Raise verified-evidence density per thematic section WITHOUT relaxing the fold-in seam (keep leaked_[CITE:ev]=0, keep dropping unverified sentences). Then re-score on RACE task 72 and confirm Insight climbs toward 0.45-0.50; commit + append the dated WHEEL_PROGRESS.md line.

did: Diagnosed the INSIGHT lever: on the task-72 outline the cross-study synthesis section is starved to 7 evidence rows while sibling body sections hold 165 unique rows, so it could only quote review-of-reviews sources instead of comparing the primary [ev]-backed figures.

Added a GENERAL, topic-agnostic, DEFAULT-OFF structural lever in src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py: _is_cross_study_synthesis_section (structural role detector: 'synthes*' + a cross-study/contradiction/convergence/agree-disagree/reconcile signal; no topic or benchmark title hardcoded), gated by PG_SYNTHESIS_QUANT_DIRECTIVE.

(a) Evidence enrichment before the section-dispatch split: the structurally-detected synthesis section is enriched with sibling-body NUMERIC rows (_row_carries_number = contains a digit), own rows preserved first, bounded by PG_SYNTHESIS_ENRICH_CAP (default 48).

(b) Quantification directive injected into _call_section's system prompt for that same section: compare figures on the SAME dimension across DIFFERENT sources, state the range, name convergence vs conflict, cite every compared [ev_XXX] in one sentence; the block RESTATES (never relaxes) the no-derived-number gate (a number must appear verbatim in an evidence direct_quote).
Enabled PG_SYNTHESIS_QUANT_DIRECTIVE in compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py as a general lever.

Added tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_synthesis_insight_directive.py (8 tests: structural detector, default-off flag, numeric filter, enrichment selection preserves-existing/numeric-only/cap, no-op without synthesis role, directive restates faithfulness rule).

result.needs_fable: Isolation-design judgment: because the step2 0.4192 baseline was produced on a now-overwritten 1120-row corpus version, I am measuring the directive as flag-ON (treatment) minus flag-OFF (control) on the identical current corrected 997-row corpus. But the agentic OUTLINE re-runs live each compose (different react turns, different retrieved evidence, possibly different synthesis-section title/rows), so run-to-run outline variance could rival or exceed the directive's effect on Insight. Is treatment-minus-control on one paired run a trustworthy attribution, or do I need to hold the outline FIXED (reuse one run's outline JSON and only toggle the synthesis-section prompt+evidence) to cleanly credit the Insight delta — and is a single paired run enough, or should I average 2-3?

result.gap_to_top: The headline measurement is missing: both step3 composes (treatment PID 1197578, control PID 1197725) are still mid-run (outline react loop at 02:43, ~13 min in), outputs/step3_synth_insight and step3_control are empty, and no RACE score exists for the Insight lever this round. Prior best is step2 OVERALL 0.4317 / Insight 0.4192 on a now-overwritten corpus; the claim that the synthesis directive lifts Insight toward 0.45-0.50 is unproven until score_report_race.py runs on both new reports. result.next_step: Let both live composes finish (do NOT restart them). Then: (1) grep outputs/step3_treatment_compose.log for the two mechanism lines ('INSIGHT: cross-study synthesis evidence enrichment' and 'quantification directive injected') plus faithfulness PASS, and confirm the control log has NEITHER; (2) run scripts/score_report_race.py on BOTH reports. Attribution decision rule (answering Opus's question): a single paired run with live outlines AND live retrieval is not clean attribution for a small delta — but it need not be redone if the delta is big. If treatment-minus-control Insight >= ~+0.05 AND the treatment synthesis section visibly contains multi-source range sentences citing 2+ [ev_XXX] per sentence, credit the directive, commit outputs + WHEEL_PROGRESS.md dated line with both real numbers, done. If the delta is < ~0.03 or negative, do NOT average 2-3 full agentic runs (expensive, still confounded); instead build the fixed-outline ablation: add a --reuse-outline path to scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py (it already persists multi_section_outline.json at line 222) that loads the treatment run's outline JSON and skips the react loop, then toggle only PG_SYNTHESIS_QUANT_DIRECTIVE — this removes both confounders (outline + retrieved pool) at once. Also, small hygiene edit before committing results: neutralize the AI/labor-flavored example list in _SYNTHESIS_QUANT_BLOCK (src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:984, 'share of jobs exposed/at risk... wage/employment effect, adoption rate') to domain-neutral examples ('an effect size, a prevalence or adoption percentage, a cost, a measured gain') — the operator flagged benchmark-tuning and those examples are the benchmark task's domain.

Verdict CONTINUE with a concrete decision rule for the attribution question.

A clean fixed-POOL ablation was NOT built: the compose script does not persist the full live-retrieved evidence pool (only outline JSON), so reusing the outline JSON alone would NOT control the pool confound — a true ablation needs adding pool-persistence + a reload path to the 11k-line generator and re-running (expensive)

result.needs_fable: Given the paired A/B is confounded (treatment report 588 words shorter from a different react outline+pool) yet shows ZERO positive signal (-0.04 Insight, worse on every axis) AND control's synthesis section already produces comparable cross-study convergence/contradiction content without the directive: do you rule (a) DROP the STEP 3 quant-directive lever entirely (revert 31f488e, keep only the general structural skeleton + hygiene, lock in the measured 0.4447 structural win as the deliverable), or (b) spend a full round building a clean fixed-POOL ablation (add evidence-pool persistence to the compose script + an outline+pool reload path into the 11k-line generator, then re-run toggling only the flag) before deciding? My recommendation is (a): the structural fix at RACE 0.4447/Insight 0.4293 is the durable general win, and the lever's own synthesis section shows no edge — but the drop-vs-ablate call is yours.

result.gap_to_top: No gap against the mission's own bar: all four success criteria are met and MEASURED — cp4_used=agentic on the full corrected corpus (both arms, zero fallback), topic-appropriate synthesis-enabling structure (verified in control_report.md headers), generality proven across AI/labor + medical + finance (outline_generality_probe.json), and RACE Comprehensiveness 0.4569 / Instruction 0.4587 far above the 0.30 best-compose baseline, with the structural win reproduced twice (0.4317 step2, 0.4447 control) and beating the claude-3-7-sonnet reference (0.4218). Faithfulness hard gate PASS both arms (0 leaked [CITE:ev], strict_verify untouched). Residual headroom (readability 0.4310 lowest dim; 0.5 = judge parity) is beyond the stated bar and chasing it risks the exact benchmark-tuning the operator flagged.

Do NOT build the fixed-pool ablation — a refuted lever with zero positive signal does not justify a round of persistence plumbing.
result.reason: Every number Opus reported is real and matches the committed artifacts (per-dim RACE scores in control_race.log/treatment_race.log, 0 leaked CITE tokens in both reports, default-OFF getenv guard, 6-line domain-neutral hygiene diff). The A/B answered the question decisively despite the confound: negative on every axis with a mechanism-level check showing control's synthesis section already delivers the content the directive was meant to force — that is a refuted hypothesis, not an ambiguous one worth an expensive ablation. The durable, general, mission-aligned deliverable (topic-driven skeleton, RACE 0.4447, generality across 3 domains, faithfulness intact) is verified and reproduced; sign off with the lever removal as the closing hygiene action.
